test_description: Compare the two-line segment as a standalone entry versus merged with the following خلاف discussion.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether the line's value is mostly standalone or mostly attached.
why_this_test_matters: The owner-faithful answer depends on not overstating standalone value.

test_description: Compare the line duplicated across both adjacent excerpts versus kept only once.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether duplication adds continuity or merely redundancy.
why_this_test_matters: The raw comments explicitly favor duplication under some conditions.

test_description: Test a short line that is harmless but useless and see whether the system preserves it anyway.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: A naive policy that mistakes shortness for sufficient value.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly rejects shortness-alone reasoning.

test_description: Test a short line that is harmless and structurally useful inside adjacent excerpts.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: A naive policy that drops all tiny fragments even when they improve continuity.
why_this_test_matters: This case is exactly about structural value that is easy to dismiss.

test_description: Check whether the presence of full context falsely inflates claims about standalone value.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Context laundering, where attached value is misreported as autonomous value.
why_this_test_matters: The prompt explicitly warns against this flattening.

test_description: Inspect whether user preference for long or short excerpts is influencing the verdict.
why_this_test_matters: The raw comments loudly reject preference control.

test_description: Drop the segment entirely and compare study continuity against versions where it is retained.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether the line really is disposable or whether its loss creates subtle structural damage.
why_this_test_matters: The owner answer preserves some value even while criticizing the segment.

test_description: Attach the line to the previous excerpt versus the following excerpt and compare where its value is strongest.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: The difference between backward-retained and forward-retained value.
why_this_test_matters: The surrounding context can affect packaging without changing the final qualified A.

test_description: Force a flat A and then force a flat B, and compare both against the raw tension in the owner's comments.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether the final answer is misrepresenting the owner's actual mixed reaction.
why_this_test_matters: Preserving the tension is one of the central requirements of the prompt.
